data_notes: Rollup values were treated as authoritative for 7-day and 28-day comparisons; daily detail was used only to describe the recent sessions.
All missing recovery values and any -1-style missing values were treated as absent and excluded from averages.
Weekday training respects the lifestyle constraint: flat road runs or cycling only during the week, trail running only on Saturday or Sunday.
The athlete’s profile shows a strong trailrunning background, but recent load and current readiness were used to cap the next 28 days rather than historical peaks.
No activity date conflicts were observed in the provided recent daily detail.
Readiness status was set from deterministic readiness_and_risk_forecast.json.
Guardrails: enforced ramp rate (max_ramp_pct=10.0%). last7_hours=5.124, allowed_hours=5.636, planned_hours=5.200.

meta.lifestyle_notes: Only flat road runs or cycling allowed during the week days. Trail runs limited to Saturday or Sunday only.

days.purpose: Start the block with low-impact aerobic consistency. If you feel unusually flat, keep this to 30-35 min and leave out any extra strides.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min very easy jog with leg swings to settle the legs. Main set: 25 min relaxed flat-road run at conversational pace. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk plus light calf and hip mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, easy aerobic. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Add aerobic volume with minimal impact and keep the running legs fresh for Friday quality and the weekend trail run. If fatigue builds, keep the whole ride easy and skip the cadence surges.
days.title: Aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin to raise temperature and check leg freshness. Main set: 25 min steady aerobic ride with 3 x 3 min high-cadence efforts, separated by 3 min easy spin. Cool-down: 10 min very easy spin. Target: RPE 3-4/10, Zone 2 feel. Terrain/equipment: flat road or stationary bike.

days.purpose: Keep one quality stimulus in the week without pushing beyond current readiness. If fatigue appears mid-week, convert this to 30-40 min easy running and keep the weekend trail run fully aerobic.
days.title: Controlled tempo run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog with drills and 4 short relaxed pickups. Main set: 3 x 8 min controlled tempo on flat road with 3 min easy jog between reps. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog and light stretching. Target: RPE 6-7/10, controlled tempo effort. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Use the weekend trail window for specificity while keeping the session well within current load. Stay conservative on descents so the next week starts fresh.
days.title: Long trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog on gentle terrain to open the stride. Main set: 60 min easy trail running with smooth climbing and controlled descending, keeping effort conversational the whole time. Cool-down: 20 min very easy jog/walk plus mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, aerobic. Terrain/equipment: Saturday trail run, moderate rolling trail, about 200-250 m of gain.

days.purpose: Absorb the first build week and protect the next microcycle. Keep the day truly easy rather than sneaking in extra volume.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Warm-up: none. Main set: none. Cool-down: optional 10-15 min easy walk and gentle mobility only if stiff. Target: full recovery, RPE 1-2/10. Terrain/equipment: off-feet.

days.purpose: Restart the legs after the rest day and keep the volume aerobic. If the weekend trail run left you heavy, shorten by 10 min and keep it purely easy.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min very easy jog and ankle mobility. Main set: 25 min relaxed flat-road run, staying smooth and light. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and brief calf work. Target: RPE 3-4/10, easy aerobic. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Maintain trail-running durability without adding much fatigue. If you feel worn down, cut the circuit to one lighter round and finish early.
days.title: Strength session
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin or brisk walk with dynamic mobility. Main set: 20 min circuit of split squats, calf raises, step-ups, dead bugs, side planks, and glute bridges for 2-3 rounds with perfect control. Cool-down: 5 min easy stretching and breathing. Target: RPE 5-6/10, submax strength. Terrain/equipment: bodyweight or light load at home or gym.

days.purpose: Set up the second build week with steady aerobic work. Keep the effort honest-easy so Friday quality and Saturday trail work stay sharp.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min very easy jog with leg swings. Main set: 30 min relaxed flat-road run at conversational pace. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and light mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, easy aerobic. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Keep the aerobic load moving without pounding the legs. If the week feels heavy, make this an uninterrupted easy spin only.
days.title: Aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 25 min steady aerobic ride with 3 x 3 min high-cadence efforts, separated by 3 min easy. Cool-down: 10 min very easy spin. Target: RPE 3-4/10, Zone 2 feel. Terrain/equipment: flat road or stationary bike.

days.purpose: Progress the quality by extending the sustained work slightly while keeping the effort controlled. If fatigue appears mid-week, cut this to 2 reps and keep the rest of the day easy.
days.title: Controlled tempo run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog with drills and 4 short strides. Main set: 3 x 10 min controlled tempo on flat road with 2 min easy jog between reps. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog and light stretching. Target: RPE 6-7/10, controlled tempo effort. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Add a small step in trail duration and vertical without making the weekend a race. Keep the pace controlled enough that Sunday remains a true rest day.
days.title: Long trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog on gentle terrain. Main set: 65 min easy trail run, keeping climbs relaxed and descents smooth. Cool-down: 20 min very easy jog/walk plus mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, aerobic. Terrain/equipment: Saturday trail run, moderate rolling trail, about 250-300 m of gain.

days.purpose: Absorb the build and protect the next block. Keep this completely off-feet if the Saturday trail run leaves any lingering soreness.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Warm-up: none. Main set: none. Cool-down: optional 10-15 min easy walk and gentle mobility only if stiff. Target: full recovery, RPE 1-2/10. Terrain/equipment: off-feet.

days.purpose: Begin the next week with comfortable volume after the rest day. If the weekend trail load lingers, stay at the easy end of the pace range.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min very easy jog and mobility. Main set: 25 min relaxed flat-road run at conversational pace. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and calf/hip mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, easy aerobic. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Support tissue durability as the running workload builds. If you feel flat, make it a short mobility and core session only.
days.title: Strength session
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin or brisk walk with dynamic mobility. Main set: 22 min circuit of split squats, calf raises, step-ups, dead bugs, side planks, and glute bridges for 2-3 rounds with good form. Cool-down: 8 min easy stretching and breathing. Target: RPE 5-6/10, submax strength. Terrain/equipment: bodyweight or light load at home or gym.

days.purpose: Keep the load consistent while avoiding unnecessary strain before the Friday quality session. Stay smooth rather than trying to force pace.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min very easy jog with leg swings. Main set: 30 min relaxed flat-road run at conversational pace. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and light mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, easy aerobic. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Use cycling to support aerobic development while keeping impact low. If fatigue is present, ride steady and ignore the cadence bursts.
days.title: Aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 25 min steady aerobic ride with 3 x 3 min high-cadence efforts, separated by 3 min easy. Cool-down: 10 min very easy spin. Target: RPE 3-4/10, Zone 2 feel. Terrain/equipment: flat road or stationary bike.

days.purpose: This is the highest-quality run of the block, but it should still feel controlled rather than aggressive. If you are carrying fatigue mid-week, reduce to 3 reps and keep the final rep easy-to-moderate.
days.title: Controlled tempo run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog with drills and 4 short strides. Main set: 4 x 8 min controlled tempo on flat road with 2 min easy jog between reps. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog and light stretching. Target: RPE 6-7/10, controlled tempo effort. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Extend the weekend trail exposure a little more while still staying in base-development territory. Keep the effort easy enough that the Sunday rest day feels earned rather than necessary.
days.title: Long trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog on gentle terrain. Main set: 70 min easy trail run, keeping climbs relaxed and descents smooth. Cool-down: 20 min very easy jog/walk plus mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, aerobic. Terrain/equipment: Saturday trail run, moderate rolling trail, about 300-350 m of gain.

days.purpose: Consolidate the biggest week of the block and let the legs rebound. If soreness lingers, keep all non-essential movement light and short.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Warm-up: none. Main set: none. Cool-down: optional 10-15 min easy walk and gentle mobility only if stiff. Target: full recovery, RPE 1-2/10. Terrain/equipment: off-feet.

days.purpose: Return to running gently after the rest day and begin the recovery/consolidation week. Keep this easy even if the legs feel good.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min very easy jog and mobility. Main set: 25 min relaxed flat-road run at conversational pace. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and light calf work. Target: RPE 3-4/10, easy aerobic. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Maintain durability while the running volume eases slightly. If the week has already felt heavy, trim the final round and stop at fresh form.
days.title: Strength session
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin or brisk walk with dynamic mobility. Main set: 25 min circuit of split squats, calf raises, step-ups, dead bugs, side planks, and glute bridges for 2-3 rounds with clean form. Cool-down: 10 min easy stretching and breathing. Target: RPE 5-6/10, submax strength. Terrain/equipment: bodyweight or light load at home or gym.

days.purpose: Start the recovery week with lighter volume and fresh legs. Keep the pace relaxed and avoid the temptation to squeeze in extra work.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min very easy jog with leg swings. Main set: 20 min relaxed flat-road run at conversational pace. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and brief mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, easy aerobic. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Keep aerobic support in place while the week trends down. If you feel any fatigue, make the whole ride easy and skip the cadence work.
days.title: Aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 20 min steady aerobic ride with 2 x 3 min high-cadence efforts, separated by 3 min easy spin. Cool-down: 10 min very easy spin. Target: RPE 3-4/10, Zone 2 feel. Terrain/equipment: flat road or stationary bike.

days.purpose: A small aerobic stimulus keeps the rhythm without adding stress in the deload week. If you feel tired, make this a pure easy run and leave out the steadier middle.
days.title: Aerobic road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog and mobility. Main set: 25 min steady but conversational flat-road running, staying smooth rather than fast. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and light stretching. Target: RPE 3-4/10, aerobic support. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Consolidation week trail touch: keep the specificity, but lower the total stress. This should feel like a controlled maintenance outing rather than a big effort.
days.title: Long trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog on gentle terrain. Main set: 50 min easy trail run with relaxed climbing and smooth descending. Cool-down: 20 min very easy jog/walk plus mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10, aerobic. Terrain/equipment: Saturday trail run, moderate rolling trail, about 150-200 m of gain.

days.purpose: Use the extra recovery day to absorb the down week. Keep it quiet and avoid adding hidden load through errands or extra exercise.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Warm-up: none. Main set: none. Cool-down: optional 10-15 min easy walk and gentle mobility only if stiff. Target: full recovery, RPE 1-2/10. Terrain/equipment: off-feet.

days.purpose: Keep a touch of running rhythm without rebuilding fatigue. If the trail run left your legs dull, shorten to 25 min total and keep it purely easy.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min very easy jog with mobility. Main set: 15 min relaxed flat-road run, staying light and smooth. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and brief stretching. Target: RPE 3-4/10, easy aerobic. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Finish the block with a small durability dose and leave the athlete ready for the next build. If you feel run down, make this mobility-only and stop early.
days.title: Strength session
days.workout: Warm-up: 8 min easy spin or brisk walk with dynamic mobility. Main set: 17 min circuit of split squats, calf raises, step-ups, dead bugs, side planks, and glute bridges for 2 rounds with clean form. Cool-down: 5 min easy stretching and breathing. Target: RPE 5-6/10, submax strength. Terrain/equipment: bodyweight or light load at home or gym.

actions: Target 8-9 hours of sleep nightly and keep the schedule consistent, because the last 7 days had no usable sleep, HRV, or resting-HR telemetry.
Fuel Friday quality and Saturday trail sessions with carbs before and after; do not underfuel the long run.
Hydrate after rides and trail runs, and add sodium if the weather is warm or sweat rate is high.
Use 10-15 minutes of mobility after easy days and keep the weekly strength work controlled, not fatiguing.

risks.message: Recovery telemetry is missing for the last 7 days, so fatigue management has to rely on training load and subjective feel rather than sleep/HRV/RHR.
Recent 7-day load is elevated versus the recent baseline and overreach risk is moderate, so a second hard day or an aggressive long run would be too much right now.
Historical trailrunning capacity is much larger than the current week, but the plan deliberately stays conservative and does not try to match old peak loads.

snapshot.notes: Yesterday was a 5 km run in 28 min; the last 7 days were bike-dominant with 2 rides and 2 runs, 11.816 load-hours, and no usable sleep/HRV/RHR telemetry. The 28-day baseline is much larger and includes 14 rides, 7 runs, and 3 trail runs. Historical profile shows trailrunning as the primary discipline with much larger past capacities, but current planning stays anchored to recent load.
